events.title: Over half of South Sudan’s population faces acute hunger crisis
events.summary: Conflict and displacement are intensifying South Sudan’s hunger crisis, with 7.8 million people facing high levels of acute food insecurity while 2.2 million children are suffering from acute malnutrition, according to a joint statement on...

events.title: From Hormuz to Lebanon, crisis reverberates through trade routes, upending humanitarian networks
events.summary: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to send shockwaves through global food systems, the UN Food and Agriculture Organization has warned, as a widening crisis across the Middle East continues to strain humanitarian operations and d...

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns
events.summary: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with debate focusing on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan.UN senior political...

events.title: Darfur: Two decades on, a new generation of children faces 'horrific violence'
events.summary: Twenty years after the conflict in Darfur first sparked global outrage, children in the region are once again trapped in a catastrophic cycle of violence, hunger, and displacement – but this time, the world is failing to take notice.

events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan drone attacks condemned, South Sudan violence, airstrikes in Ukraine, South Africa Freedom Day
events.summary: The United Nations has condemned two recent drone attacks in Sudan, one of which left seven dead, Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Monday during his regular media briefing in New York.

events.title: As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief
events.summary: With top diplomats gathered at UN Headquarters to review the 1970 Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT), the UN Secretary-General warned that it must evolve to survive the age of AI and other new technologies.

events.title: Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns
events.summary: Nearly 7.5 million children across the Central Sahel region in Africa are in urgent need of humanitarian assistance – “an emergency that remains too far from the attention of the international community,” a senior official with the UN chil...

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council
events.summary: The recently extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon remains under strain as violence continues, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Israeli strikes over the weekend. Meanwhile, the Security Council is debating maritime sec...

events.title: Chokepoints and conflict: How the Hormuz crisis is exposing global shipping vulnerabilities
events.summary: The blockading of ships in the Strait of Hormuz as a result of the conflict between the United States and Iran has demonstrated how ships and seafarers have become “leverage in geopolitical disputes,” according to the head of the UN’s Inte...

events.title: Mali: Guterres calls for international solutions to curb spread of violent extremism in the Sahel
events.summary: There are reports of continuing clashes in Mali on Sunday, a day after a series of coordinated attacks across the landlocked African nation against Government forces by extremists and northern separatist rebels.

events.title: South Pacific islanders strive to rescue their home from toxic legacy
events.summary: More than 80 years after World War Two, the Solomon Islands remain one of the most heavily mine-contaminated places in the Pacific.

events.title: Rising nuclear risks put global security pact to the test at UN Headquarters
events.summary: At a moment of mounting nuclear danger and deepening global mistrust, governments from nearly every country have convened at United Nations Headquarters to confront a central question: will the world’s main barrier against the spread of nu...

events.title: World News in Brief: Airliner safety warning, drought in Somalia, solar boosts farming in Ethiopia
events.summary: The head of the UN’s civil aviation agency, ICAO, has called for immediate global action to protect commercial flights from escalating military threats, including missiles, drones, and GPS signal jamming.

events.title: As Gaza misery continues, shortages in Iran begin to bite
events.summary: Desperate and dangerous conditions in Gaza continue to hamper recovery efforts for the wartorn enclave's people, the UN health agency said on Friday, while demining experts warned that they’ve “barely scratched the surface” in assessing th...

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 24 April: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended as humanitarian concerns persist
events.summary: The United States has announced that the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire will be extended by three weeks following talks in Washington, offering a potential pause in hostilities. Meanwhile, humanitarian concerns are growing over shortages in Iran...

events.title: World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update
events.summary: Continued strikes, shelling and gunfire in residential areas of Gaza are raising alarm over civilian safety, the UN relief coordination office OCHA has warned.

events.title: Devastation at every turn greets UN aid mission to south Lebanon
events.summary: A UNICEF relief mission reached a large hospital in the southern Lebanese town of Tebnine on Thursday, only to find massive destruction and staggering needs.

events.title: Despite ceasefire, Hormuz tensions continue to throttle supply chains worldwide
events.summary: A temporary ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been extended, offering an opening for diplomacy – but persistent tensions in the Strait of Hormuz are compounding trade disruptions and intensifying pressure on humanitarian ope...

events.title: Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch
events.summary: The shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz caused by war in the Middle East has exposed a new threat: a looming shortage of strategic minerals that drive economies all over the world – and a race by countries to obtain them.

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence
events.summary: The Security Council is addressing the deepening crisis in Haiti on Thursday, centered on the Secretary-General’s latest report which highlights a security landscape of both intensified enforcement and rising civilian risk. While operation...
